result.status_detail: QUIC flag=Disabled, chat.mistral.ai requests show h2 (no h3/Q050)
result.verified_after_relaunch: Disabled (dropdown shows 'Disabled' with blue non-default indicator dot; flag title highlighted orange)
result.key_observation: QUIC flag was ALREADY Disabled from a prior configuration session on this Test PC. Selecting 'Disabled' again did not produce a Relaunch banner -- expected Chrome behavior when the value is unchanged. The live chat.mistral.ai check (3/3 requests on h2) confirms QUIC is in fact disabled at the wire level.

screenshots_inventory.description: chrome://flags/#enable-quic showing 'Experimental QUIC protocol' = Disabled, with non-default blue dot indicator and orange title highlight
DevTools Network panel filtered by 'mistral' showing 3 chat.mistral.ai requests (manifest.webmanifest, version.current, user.limits) all with Protocol = h2

Initial state captured immediately after selecting Disabled in the QUIC dropdown -- no Relaunch banner visible at bottom (confirms no value change occurred, flag was already Disabled prior to this session)

result.diagnostic_for_dev_team: Cycle 94 R-21 success: Chrome on Test PC has QUIC disabled. APF intercept layer should now see all chat.mistral.ai / you.com / similar AI service traffic on TCP/H2 (instead of UDP/H3 bypass). Re-running #599 mistral_jsonpatch_v3 verify with QUIC disabled is now meaningful -- the previous bypass diagnosis there may have been the QUIC bypass issue rather than a URL pattern / cert / content-type problem. Recommend immediate re-test of #599 (or #600 gamma_sse_v2 with v2.1 CORS fix) to confirm intercept layer is healthy now.
result.request_to_revisit: Suggested follow-up requests for dev to push: (a) re-run #599 mistral PII verify (without changing v3 envelope) to see if intercept now fires; (b) build gamma_sse_v2.1 with ACAC:true header and re-run #600. Both of these were diagnosed as 'APF bypass / CORS reject' but those diagnoses were made WITH QUIC enabled -- some/all of the bypass may have been QUIC-via-UDP rather than envelope/cert problems.
obstacles_encountered: QUIC flag was already in Disabled state from a prior configuration; selecting Disabled again did not produce a Relaunch banner -- expected Chrome behavior when re-selecting the same flag value, and does not indicate failure. The live mistral.ai HTTP/2 verification confirms QUIC is in fact disabled.

notes: Cycle 94 R-21 Chrome QUIC disable on Test PC: SUCCESS. Flag was already Disabled from prior session (no relaunch needed). Live chat.mistral.ai verification: 3/3 requests on h2 protocol (no h3/Q050). APF intercept layer should now see TCP/H2 traffic for mistral / you / any H3-advertising AI service. CRITICAL FOLLOW-UP: re-test #599 mistral_jsonpatch_v3 verify and #600 gamma_sse_v2 verify with QUIC disabled -- the previously-diagnosed APF bypass (#599) and CORS rejection (#600) may have been partially or wholly caused by QUIC-via-UDP bypass rather than envelope/cert problems. 3 screenshots in files/601/.
